Velium Hound Fur Cloak  
 


Slot: BACK
AC: 8
STR: +8 STA: +10
SV FIRE: +5 SV COLD: +5
WT: 1.9 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Velium Hound Fur Cloak

Slot: BACK
AC: 8
STR: +8 STA: +10
SV FIRE: +5 SV COLD: +5
WT: 1.9 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No
Tribute:1052
Lucy Entry By:Mynlaeni
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 08:22:31
Page Updated:Sat Feb 26th, 2011

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Average Price: No Data Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 1
[Comments ]

Crafted: This item is crafted by players.
  • Velium Hound Fur Cloak (Tailoring, 322 trivial)
    • Expansion: Not Set
    Yield: 1 x Velium Hound Fur Cloak


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
Velious
QuoteReply
# Feb 25 2011 at 3:54 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1641773856190
Scholar
2,308 posts
Needs to be marked as xpac
▲ Page top
Quite fine indeed.
QuoteReply
# Aug 02 2001 at 10:10 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592249444106
146 posts
While 8AC is not particularily amazing, for a cloak it is reasonable for a Rogue. The statistics are perfect on this item though. Stamina is notoriously difficult to find in any quantity and extra strength, plus a few saves, is always desired. I'd cheerfully pay 5K for a cloak like this, if I had 5K to spare right now. 4K is a quite reasonable price to pay. If you disagree, feel free to drag some groups out to WW and hunt the pelts. I am sure any respectable tailor will try a combine for you at a nominal fee. Your needs follow and they are rare to expensive:

4 velium hound furs, platinum thread, cloak pattern, vial of purified mana

cheers,
Celeris Tujimson - Rogue of The Nameless
▲ Page top
Tailoring
QuoteReply
# Jul 18 2001 at 10:07 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591763349237
19 posts
I laugh at anyone who thinks the would pay LESS than 3k for this from me and I AM a tailor. Don't like it? Go find some sucker to buy it from.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 21 2001 at 11:33 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Enough arguments on what it's worth =) It's worth whatever a person will pay for it...But anywayz, I'm gunna go swimming, since its my birthday and all :) Finally 16! hehe...I will gladly except ANY birthday gifts =)
recipe?
QuoteReply
# Jun 12 2001 at 5:29 AM Rating: Decent

#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 12 2001 at 12:54 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) You people should die. You people should die and burn...
#Anonymous, Posted: May 30 2001 at 3:16 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) 1200PP IS A FAIR PRICE . A GOOD TAILOR CAN MAKE THIS ITEM AND INGRDIANTS ONLY COST A FRACTION OF THE PRICE. SURE THE THE FURS MIGHT BE TOUGH TO GET.. WELL KINDA.. BUT THATS WHAT HIGH LVL FRIENDS ARE FOR. STILL THOUGH SPIDER FUR AND HBC ARE BETTER IMO. CAN GET SPIDER FUR FOR 2K ON KARANA AND HBC 6K SO NOWAY IS THIS WORTH MORE THAN 1500 TOPS
RE: common logic
QuoteReply
# Jun 05 2001 at 7:13 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591760259329
12 posts
Its intersting how players who write in capital letters usually have so little to say... and know so little about the real facts.
*nods*
▲ Page top
RE: common logic
QuoteReply
# May 30 2001 at 6:34 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591750127954
9 posts
Being that I AM a tailor, and considering the fact that not only are the pieces a ROYAL pain in the behind to aquire, BUT the trivial level is 250 plus and believe me when I say that the tailor can EASILY blow 5k plat PLUS days and weeks of trying to aquire all the components, just trying to get a single point raise... so take your

Quote
--------------------------------------------
"SO NOWAY IS THIS WORTH MORE THAN 1500 TOPS"
--------------------------------------------

and stick it in that highly uncomfortable place where ogres fear to tread...
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 04 2001 at 4:02 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) the jeweler has it right on the aspect that ppl pay what its worth. im NOT a tailor, nor do i plan to be. i AM, however a regular customer for tailors. BOTTOM LINE -- I DONT CARE WHAT IT COST YOU TO MAKE. i just want the most for MY money!!
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 01 2001 at 7:08 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) try jewling once if you think you have it bad, more in the 20k area, and no you cant buy all the gems, im sure you have seen blue diamonds selling for 2k+ heh. if you complain about your tradeskill its your own damn fault, people will pay what it is WORtH.
RE: common logic
QuoteReply
# Jul 02 2001 at 11:12 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592246918785
117 posts
Actually it took me about 30k to max out jc. Tailoring is by far the hardest tradeskill though. You can't buy the stuff from a vendor so not only is it costly but it's a royal pain in the ***** This is a great cloak. I'd gladly pay 4k for one. Give me a tell if you're a master tailor on tunare, I have a few things that I'd like to buy and plenty of cash to buy them with.

Nods

considering that Plat Jade tops out jewelcrafting with 250, if your using velium or the high gems to practice with your an idiot

Nice
QuoteReply
# May 29 2001 at 3:59 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757381256
26 posts
IMO, I'd say a ball park figure on Tarrew Marr would be about 8K, it's a mighty fine Cloak. Especially for Damage dealers like Rogues. If it "was" only going for like 2K, I'd buy one myself. IMHO, it's just another way the trade skills are getting a shot in the arm, Class speicific Armors from Tailoring are also awesome. WTG VI.. Wait a minute.. hmm, I'll have to rethink that statement.. but deffinitely a step forward for them, hehehe.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 29 2001 at 6:29 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ok first of all.
#Anonymous, Posted: May 29 2001 at 4:41 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) It seems that Tailoring has now become very useful as jewel crafting has always been. When will Blacksmithing catch up? I mean sure Blacksmithing has a few decent items for under 20 people but NOTHING a blacksmith can make comes anywhere near this cloak. N_O_T_H_I_N_G
RE: Curious
QuoteReply
# Sep 10 2001 at 3:42 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591751003709
View User Forum
120 posts
Enchanted full plate armor has like 14 ac on the cloak, i think. and depending on the metal bar you use like 4 str, wis, sta, or another stat i cant remember int maybe. Its an awsome armor set if you can find the griffene blood. This item however is pretty good, you give up ac over plate cloaks, but its sta is a really good thing. Weather or not someone would give up 4 ac for 4 more str 6 more sta over a spider fur is up to them.

Also yes, things get sold for what people will pay, to a point. Say a tailor spends a week collecting to suceed on an item and puts 1k into it. You think your gona get that item for 1200pp your just kidding yourself. Its called economy, people will take extra time to sell something for more so they make a profit instead of pissing there money into the wind. Im unshur about the economy on any server but the tibunal, but i would take a guess that 4k is a pretty reasonable price, and spider fur is only 2.5k on trib.

There is alot of people flaming others couse they say a price, it all depends on your server, a price on an old server will be low, a price on a new server will be high.
▲ Page top
RE: Curious
QuoteReply
# Aug 18 2001 at 12:12 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765736404
2 posts
I think most cultural cloaks beet this thing by 4-6 ac,

# Sep 29 2001 at 6:33 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Yeah, for plate wearing classes the AC is better. Still I like the stats and saves on the velium hound. Imbued Field Plate of Rallos Zek is a pretty specific item, mainly gonna be large race warriors wearing this. The velium hound is all/all making it a much better item to players in general.

QuoteReply
# May 28 2001 at 10:22 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
HBC > Velium Hound Fur Cloak

...except for monks maybe due to weight, and this is still a bit heavy there if you look at CoS and spider fur cloak.

10 STA comes out to ~50 hps (ranger, level 57).

No way in hell I'd give up 2 AC for 5 hps, 3 str and minor saves.

It's nice, but it ain't uber.
▲ Page top
common ingrediants
QuoteReply
# May 26 2001 at 4:58 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
well, the ingrediants are NOT hard to get, the price however is probably a bit low, i would NOT pay more than 2k for this item, go to west wastes, tons of velium hounds out there, and yes, their fur is a common drop. the rest of the stuff can be bought in thurgadin near bank.
